task: Fix macOS SSL certificate verification error in Discord webhook client and establish per-repo webhook configuration strategy
discussion_summary: Friend encountered SSL certificate verification failure on macOS when Discord webhook tried to send. Root cause: Python on macOS doesn't use system certificates by default. Applied `certifi` library fix to both `send_message` and `send_file` methods in discord.py. User then raised critical architectural question: how should askr differentiate between repos and support different webhooks per repo, since current design stores keys globally in `~/.config/askr/.env`. This exposed a gap between the single-key-per-machine model and the user's need for private vs. official project separation.

accomplishments.what: Added certifi SSL context to discord.py send_message method
Added certifi SSL context to discord.py send_file method
Committed SSL fix and verified install.sh will auto-install certifi

next_actions.action: Design per-repo webhook configuration system: decide between (a) repo-level .env override in askr_state/, (b) team/project scoping in Phase 4 structure, or (c) environment variable per-repo pattern
next_actions.why: User explicitly needs different webhooks for private vs official projects; current global-only design blocks this use case

next_actions.action: Update askr init to optionally prompt for repo-specific webhook URL if not found in global config
next_actions.why: Enables per-repo differentiation without breaking existing single-key workflows

next_actions.action: Document in CLAUDE.md or README how to set repo-specific Discord webhooks for team/project separation
next_actions.why: User needs clear guidance on the intended workflow for managing multiple projects

next_actions.action: Verify friend's SSL fix works: git pull, re-run install.sh, askr init, test Discord send
next_actions.why: Unblock immediate blocker before architectural changes

decisions.decision: Global keys in ~/.config/askr/.env are shared across all repos on a machine
decisions.reason: Simplest initial design; setup happens once, not per-project

decisions.decision: setup_keys() only runs when ~/.config/askr/.env doesn't exist
decisions.reason: Avoid re-prompting on every askr init; keys are machine-level, not project-level

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Global keys are sufficient for all use cases; askr init just sets up state files and hooks
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: then how will askr differentiate between repo's? also, what if I want to have different webhooks for differnt repo's?

failed_approaches.approach: Assuming single global webhook URL works for all repos
failed_approaches.reason: User has legitimate need to separate private and official projects with different Discord webhooks

relational_files.why: Calls discord.py send methods; error messages now propagate SSL details
Contains setup_keys() and askr init logic; needs update for per-repo webhook support
Runs pip install -r requirements.txt; will auto-install certifi after this session's commit
Phase 4 team scoping may address per-repo/per-team webhook differentiation

blockers: Architectural decision needed: how to support per-repo webhook URLs without breaking global key model
